q: How often do you buy shoes?
examiner_note: Nói về tần suất và lý do. Sử dụng 'wear out', 'tempted', 'investment', 'longevity'.

band7.text: I don’t buy shoes very often. Normally, I only get a new pair once or twice a year unless my old ones wear out quickly or there’s a really good sale that I can’t resist. I prefer to have a few good pairs rather than many cheap ones.
band7.note: Trả lời thực tế, sử dụng từ vựng tốt (wear out, resist).

band8.text: I’m not a frequent shoe shopper. I usually only purchase a new pair when my current ones are showing signs of wear and tear or if I need a specific type of footwear for an upcoming occasion. I prioritize quality over quantity and look for shoes that offer both comfort and longevity.
band8.note: Sử dụng từ vựng hay (frequent, wear and tear, upcoming, longevity).

band9.text: My shoe purchases are quite infrequent and purposeful. I view footwear as a long-term investment, so I’m willing to spend more on high-quality pairs that will last for several years. I only buy when absolutely necessary, often waiting for specific releases or high-end brands that I know align with my style and comfort requirements.
band9.note: Sử dụng từ vựng ở mức độ cao (infrequent, purposeful, investment, align with).

v.example: The car shows some signs of wear and tear.
The longevity of the battery is impressive.

advanced_vocab.example: She took a purposeful step towards the door.

similar_questions: What was the last pair of shoes you bought?
Do you like shopping for shoes?
